rationale: The spec is complete and right-sized for planning. AC1-AC4 plus AC-global pair observable behavior with runnable commands or named tests, and the cited seams exist at the pinned SHA. R1 covers both panel verify and panel tally, stale-target truth, missing/transient ref behavior, and reachable REJECT/incomplete/hard_block/threshold legs without changing complete. R2, R3, and R4 each have crisp proofs and stable scope. The four requirements decompose cleanly into about four independently landable beads: R1 panel CLI/facts/rendering, R2 workflow regex plus mirror, R3 create --gate writer defaults, R4 config validation pin. Non-goals correctly fence blp6/zty3 lifecycle-finalize work. No requirement relies on vague works/correctly language without an observable. Note: the requested BRIEF.md is not present in commit 0b82c1d29151e3a87e498c08622865c1deb0149e, so this review used the prompt lens plus the pinned spec.

findings.detail: AC1-AC4 and AC-global are runnable as written; their commands and named tests map to existing seams or packages at the pinned SHA.
R1 covers both verbs and the non-staleness decision legs; R3 includes invalid-gate recovery naming all five keys; R4 pins both empty-model-with-family acceptance and empty-model-alone refusal.
The scope maps cleanly to four beads with no hidden ordering dependency beyond normal same-file coordination between R1 and R3.
The lifecycle-finalize bugs mindspec-blp6 and mindspec-zty3 are explicitly excluded, and R1 preserves complete's gate semantics.
The requirements and ACs are falsifiable and specify concrete outputs, exit status, JSON fields, byte identity, or test commands.
Pinned read of BRIEF.md failed because the file is untracked/not present in commit 0b82c1d29151e3a87e498c08622865c1deb0149e.
